module_map.responsibility: Orchestrator and entry point. Coordinates file fetching, sorting, and display. Manages CLI flags, theme configuration, and caches (Git, Owner).
Metadata collection and representation. Extracts file attributes (permissions, size, date, owner, git status) from filesystem. Meta::from_path is the entry point for data extraction.
CLI argument parsing and configuration. Defines user-selectable options (blocks, colors, sorting, layout, icons, permissions display format). Configurable trait bridges CLI and config files.
Rendering engine. Converts Meta and Block selections to formatted output. Handles grid/tree layouts, padding, headers, color, hyperlinks, and symbol rendering.
Visual theme definitions. Maps file properties (type, git status, color profile) to display symbols and colors. Decouples presentation logic from data structures.
Color management and colorization. Creates Colors struct from theme/flags. Provides colorize() method to apply color codes to strings.
Icon resolution. Maps file properties to icons based on IconTheme and FileType. Interfaces with Name rendering in display output.
Sorting pipeline. Generates sort functions from SortColumn/SortOrder flags. Applied to Meta Vec in core::sort().

types_produced: FileType, Permissions, Owner, Size, Date, Name, SymLink, INode, Links, AccessControl, GitFileStatus
data_flow.description: Reads filesystem metadata (via fs::symlink_metadata, Metadata struct). Creates specialized sub-types for each attribute (permissions, size, date, etc.). Calls submodule constructors (e.g., Owner::from, Size::from, Date::from). Returns single Meta struct containing all fields.

fetch() collects paths and calls Meta::from_path for each, building Vec<Meta>. sort() creates sort function closures from flags.sorters list using sort module. Applies closures to Vec<Meta> for ordering.

types_produced: Colors (mapping from Elem enum to Color codes)
data_flow.description: Icons struct built once from flags/theme; used later to resolve file icons by FileType and Name properties. Colors struct built once from theme; colorize() method applied to strings during render() calls. Both are passed as references through display pipeline.

data_flow.key_function: get_output(meta, owner_cache, colors, icons, git_theme, flags, display_option, padding_rules, tree_context) -> Vec<String>
data_flow.description: Iterates over flags.blocks (Vec<Block>). For each block, pattern-matches Meta fields and calls their render() method (e.g., meta.size.render(), meta.name.render()). render() methods apply colors via colors.colorize(). Returns Vec<String> that are joined into a single line.

data_flow.key_function: grid() for grid layout, tree() for tree layout
data_flow.description: grid() wraps get_output for each Meta entry, collects Vec<String> into term_grid::Cells with padding rules. Calls Grid::fit_into_* to lay out columns/rows. tree() recursively renders nested directories with Tree edges. Both return final String via Grid::to_string() or String::push_str.
Core::display() calls grid() or tree() based on flags.layout. Captures String output, passes to print_output! macro which writes to stdout. Error handling via print_error! on failures.

cross_module_hubs.reason: Configuration hub. Imported by core.rs, display.rs, meta/mod.rs, color.rs, icon.rs, sort.rs, app.rs, config_file.rs. Flags struct affects all downstream logic (block selection, color theme, icon theme, sorting, permissions display, layout). Changes to Block enum or flag fields ripple through entire pipeline.

Data hub. Core imports Meta type; display.rs consumes Meta in get_output; color.rs uses meta.owner; icon.rs uses meta.name and meta.file_type; sort.rs uses Meta fields for sort predicates. Exports 10+ types (Permissions, Owner, Size, Date, Name, etc.). Any Meta schema change requires downstream refactoring.

Orchestration hub. Imports and instantiates Colors, Icons, GitTheme, Flags, GitCache; calls fetch(), sort(), display(). Coordinates sequence of operations: configure -> fetch -> sort -> display. main.rs depends on Core; Core is the single entry point for all subsystem initialization and orchestration.

change_proposal.title: Add SHA-256 file checksum display column
files_to_modify: src/flags/blocks.rs: Add Block::Checksum variant (e.g., SHA256), implement get_header(), update TryFrom
src/meta/mod.rs: Add checksum field to Meta struct, compute in from_path (sha2 crate)
src/meta/checksum.rs: New file; CheckSum type with render() method following FileSize/Date patterns
src/display.rs: Add Block::Checksum case in get_output() match statement
src/main.rs or Cargo.toml: Add optional feature flag (e.g., 'checksums' default=false) to gate sha2 dependency
src/config_file.rs: Add 'checksum' to Blocks config parsing (optional)
new_types: CheckSum struct: wraps String hash value; implements render(colors: &Colors) -> ColoredString
Block::SHA256 enum variant (or Block::CheckSum with enum variants for algorithm)
Optional CheckSumOption flag if multi-algorithm support planned
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Existing block implementation (Size, Date, Owner, Permission). Size pattern is closest: has render() method with optional padding, field wrapped in Option<T> in Meta struct, from_path calls Size::from(metadata, flags). Follow this for CheckSum::from(path) -> Result<CheckSum>.
change_proposal.integration_point: Rendering: get_output() pattern-matches Block::SHA256, calls meta.checksum.render(colors). Data collection: Meta::from_path calls CheckSum::from(path) after getting other fields. Config: flags/blocks.rs and config_file.rs parse 'checksum' string into Block::SHA256. Block enum controls visibility/ordering via Blocks vec.
risks: Performance: Computing SHA-256 on every file in large directory recursion is I/O-intensive. Mitigation: add --checksum flag (off by default); optionally cache by inode+mtime.
Schema breakage: Adding Meta::checksum field requires all code constructing Meta to provide value. Mitigation: use Option<CheckSum> to allow None for compatibility.
Dependency: sha2 crate adds build overhead. Mitigation: use feature flag; make optional.
Terminal width: Adding checksum column may exceed terminal width. Mitigation: truncate hash display to first 8-12 chars (short hash); add --checksum-length flag.
Blocking I/O: Computing hashes blocks tree traversal. Mitigation: consider rayon parallel hashing in future; for now, document performance implications in help text.

params: max_depth=2 on src/ for directory overview
focus=from_path with follow_depth=2 for data flow tracing
focus=grid with follow_depth=2 for display pipeline
rg 'use crate' src/ for cross-module dependency mapping
rg '::' core.rs to verify import statements
view src/flags/blocks.rs lines 1-250 to understand Block enum and Configurable pattern
view src/display.rs lines 326-450 to understand get_output rendering loop and block handling

analysis_summary.architecture_pattern: Modular data flow pipeline: CLI/config -> Meta collection -> sorting -> rendering -> output
analysis_summary.key_abstraction: Block enum (7 main variants: Permission, User, Group, Size, Date, Name, GitStatus) controls what fields are collected and displayed
analysis_summary.extension_readiness: High. Block enum is extensible via Blocks/TryFrom pattern. Meta struct can accept new fields. get_output() match statement is the integration point. Pattern is well-established and followed consistently (Size/Date/Owner all follow same render() interface).
